title: SageAttention2++: A More Efficient Implementation of SageAttention2
core_contribution: Uses FP16-accumulated FP8 MMA instructions with narrowed quantization ranges to achieve 3.9x speedup over FlashAttention2 with no accuracy loss.
summary: SageAttention2++ switches from FP32-accumulated FP8 instructions (2x over FP16) to FP16-accumulated FP8 instructions (4x over FP16) for PV matmul. Narrowing FP8 quantization ranges for P (224) and V (4.5) keeps accumulated products within FP16 range. Delayed FP32 Buffering halves type conversion overhead. Achieves 3.9x speedup over FlashAttention2 on RTX4090 at 32K sequence length with CosSim 99.97%.
key_findings: FP8 with FP16 accumulator is 4x faster than FP16 (vs 2x for FP32 accumulator)
Narrowing FP8 quantization ranges maintains identical accuracy (CosSim 99.97%)
Delayed FP32 Buffering halves FP16→FP32 conversion overhead
limitations: Only evaluated on NVIDIA Ada/Blackwell consumer GPUs
FP16 accumulator instruction not available on Hopper
Only optimizes PV Matmul; QK unchanged
Very short paper with limited ablation

infra_impact.algorithm: Enables faster attention without algorithmic changes
infra_impact.kernel: Demonstrates practical use of FP16-accumulated FP8 MMA; generalizable to bounded-output FP8 GEMM
infra_impact.framework: Drop-in replacement for FlashAttention2 in inference pipelines
infra_impact.llm: Directly accelerates prefill phase for long-context applications
infra_impact.agent: Reduced latency enables faster real-time inference

open_questions: Can FP16 accumulator tricks be applied to datacenter GPUs?
What is the theoretical limit of quantization range narrowing?
Can this be combined with sparse attention for multiplicative speedups?
